mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes using custom resources. It is ideal for users looking to automate monitoring configurations without deep knowledge of Prometheus. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster setups.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Prometheus Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This repository contains the kube-prometheus-stack chart.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the kube-prometheus-stack
steps.description:
This command installs the kube-prometheus-stack Helm chart in the 'monitoring' namespace, creating all necessary resources.

```bash

kubectl create namespace monitoring

helm install kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.75.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the monitoring stack.
> Replace with the desired version if necessary.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that all components of the kube-prometheus-stack are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

kubectl get svc -n monitoring

```

> Lists all pods in the monitoring namespace to ensure they are running.
> Lists all services in the monitoring namespace to verify service creation.

steps.title: Access the Prometheus UI
steps.description:
This command sets up port forwarding to access the Prometheus UI from your local machine.

```bash

kubectl port-forward svc/kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus 9090:9090 -n monitoring

```

> Access Prometheus at http://localhost:9090 after running this command.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the kube-prometheus-stack
uninstall.description:
This step removes the kube-prometheus-stack and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data.

```bash

helm uninstall kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> Uninstalls the Helm release.
> Deletes the monitoring namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the kube-prometheus-stack
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Prometheus data. This step will upgrade the Helm release to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl cp monitoring/kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-0:/prometheus /path/to/backup

helm upgrade kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.76.0

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Backup Prometheus data.
> Replace with the desired version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Prometheus" in version "monitoring.coreos.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Prometheus CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Prometheus CRDs are installed by running 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/main/bundle.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Prometheus server is taking too long to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Prometheus pod using 'kubectl logs <prometheus-pod-name> -n monitoring' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus UI not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Port forwarding may not be set up correctly or the service may not be running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running with 'kubectl get svc -n monitoring' and re-run the port-forward command.

troubleshooting.title: Alertmanager not receiving alerts
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to misconfigured alert rules or missing service monitors.

**Fix:**
Check the alert rules and service monitors configuration in the monitoring namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new alerting rules and improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review and update your alerting rules to align with the new specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see the Prometheus UI accessible at http://localhost:9090 with a healthy status and metrics being collected.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
